seoTitle: Embed Video Questions in Microsoft Forms
metaDescription: Add a YouTube or Microsoft Stream video directly into a Microsoft Forms question so respondents can watch and answer without leaving the page.
intro: Sending respondents away to an external link to watch a video before answering a question hurts completion rates and looks unpolished. Microsoft Forms lets you embed video directly next to a question, which is useful for training modules, video-based assessments, or richer feedback surveys.

steps.heading: Add a question block
steps.body: Click Add New and choose the question type you want the video attached to.

steps.heading: Open the media insert option
steps.body: Click the Insert Media icon on the right side of the question title field.

steps.heading: Choose Video and paste your link
steps.body: Select Video from the pop-up and paste a YouTube or Microsoft Stream URL into the address field.

steps.heading: Confirm the embed
steps.body: Click Add to merge the video into the question, then adjust its size using the media toolbar if needed.

steps.heading: Write the accompanying question
steps.body: Add your multi-choice or text answer fields beneath the video so respondents can answer right after watching.

faq.question: What video sources does Microsoft Forms support?
faq.answer: Forms supports embedding videos from direct YouTube links and Microsoft Stream enterprise links.

faq.question: Can I use a video as a header instead of tied to a specific question?
faq.answer: Yes, video blocks can work as standalone context elements or be paired directly with an evaluation question.

faq.question: Will the video play inline or open a new tab?
faq.answer: The video streams inline directly within the form, so respondents don't have to leave the page.

faq.question: Does the embedded video resize for mobile viewers?
faq.answer: Yes, the video layout is responsive and scales for both desktop and mobile screens.
